practically,<|12.74|> <|12.74|> there's not one part of the human body that's any more important than any other part.<|17.44|> <|18.06|> And the same thing is true for the body of Christ as a community.<|21.50|> <|21.80|> Each individual member.<|24.48|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> is an essential gift to the body's health and well-being and sense of who it is.<|7.04|> <|7.86|> He got this image from emerging physiological understandings of the human body in the Greco-Roman world.<|16.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's a powerful <|5.92|> And we're a body knit together<|8.30|> <|8.30|> and every part matters.<|10.24|> <|11.40|> And that means that we're God's body<|14.64|> <|14.64|> and because we're God's body,<|17.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We have soul. We have spirit. So as we pray and imagine our future individually or collectively,<|8.78|> <|9.22|> the most important thing to do is take a deep breath and remember that God's spirit is literally<|16.24|> <|16.24|> that breath that walks with us each step of the journey that we must make.<|20.62|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And it's when we remember our souls, it's always when we remember our souls that we find our future.<|8.68|><|endoftext|>
